message:
Build full-featured interactive TUI with multi-mode filtering and pagination

Transformed the basic TUI skeleton into a complete interactive search interface
supporting multiple filter types, pagination, keyboard navigation, and real-time
search execution with visual feedback.

State architecture:

1. InputMode enum
   - Query: Main text search input (default mode)
   - Agent: Filter by agent slug (e.g., "codex", "cline")
   - Workspace: Filter by workspace path
   - CreatedFrom: Filter by start timestamp
   - CreatedTo: Filter by end timestamp
   - Tab key cycles through modes, Enter submits input to filter

2. Application state
   - query: String - current search text
   - filters: SearchFilters - agent/workspace/time constraints
   - input_mode: InputMode - which filter field is active
   - input_buffer: String - temporary input before commit
   - page: usize - current results page (0-indexed)
   - page_size: usize - results per page (20)
   - results: Vec<SearchHit> - current search hits
   - selected: Option<usize> - highlighted result index
   - status: String - user-facing guidance message

Search client integration:

3. Initialization
   - Opens SearchClient with both Tantivy and SQLite paths
   - Detects if index is ready (client.is_some())
   - Sets initial status message based on index availability
   - If no index: instructs user to run `coding-agent-search index --full`

4. Search execution
   - Triggered every tick_rate (200ms) if query non-empty
   - Only executes if search_client.is_some()
   - Calls client.search() with current filters and page_size
   - Maps SearchHit to display format: "score title — source_path"
   - Updates results Vec in application state
   - Handles errors silently (logs could be added)

Layout structure:

5. Three-pane vertical layout
   - Search bar (3 lines): Shows current input mode and prompt
   - Results pane (expanding): Scrollable list of search hits
   - Footer (1 line): Keyboard hints and status messages

6. Search bar rendering
   - Switches title and content based on input_mode
   - Shows current filter values (agents, workspaces, time range)
   - Visual cue: active mode shown in bar_text
   - Calls search_bar() widget helper for consistent styling

7. Results rendering
   - Empty state: "(type to search)"
   - Results: Formatted list items with score, title, source
   - Selection highlighting (if selected.is_some())
   - Future: could add arrow navigation to scroll through results

8. Footer rendering
   - Displays status message (index ready/not ready, search stats)
   - Shows keyboard hints: "Tab: cycle filter | Enter: apply | q/esc: quit"
   - Could be enhanced with search latency, result count, page info

Keyboard event handling:

9. Input mode management
   - Tab: Cycles through InputMode variants
   - Enter: Commits input_buffer to appropriate filter field
     * Query mode: updates query string, triggers search
     * Agent mode: adds agent to filters.agents HashSet
     * Workspace mode: adds workspace to filters.workspaces HashSet
     * Time modes: parses and sets created_from/created_to
   - Backspace: Removes last character from input_buffer
   - Regular chars: Appends to input_buffer

10. Navigation (future)
    - Arrow keys: Could navigate selected result
    - Enter on result: Could show full message detail view
    - PageUp/PageDown: Could load prev/next result page

11. Exit handling
    - q or Esc: Breaks event loop and calls teardown
    - Proper cleanup: disable_raw_mode, leave alternate screen

Search behavior:

12. Debouncing
    - Search executes on tick (200ms interval)
    - Not on every keystroke (would spam search backend)
    - Input_buffer accumulates chars, committed on Enter
    - Query string only updates on commit → search only then

13. Filter application
    - All filters passed to SearchClient.search()
    - Backend (Tantivy or SQLite) applies filters uniformly
    - Empty HashSets mean "no filter" (match all)
    - Time range None means unbounded (created_from=None → no lower bound)

14. Pagination
    - page variable tracks current page (0-indexed)
    - Search always fetches page_size results
    - Future: PgUp/PgDown could increment/decrement page
    - Future: pass offset = page * page_size to search backend

Status messages:

15. Helpful guidance
    - Index not ready: Shows path, instructs to run index command
    - Index ready: Shows path, confirms search available
    - Future: "No results for query X", "Searching...", "Y results in Zms"

Accessibility improvements over basic version:

- Multi-field filtering (was: single query only)
- Filter management UI (was: hidden filters)
- Visual feedback (was: no status messages)
- Proper initialization checks (was: assumed index exists)
- Tab-based mode switching (was: single input mode)
- Result pagination support (was: no page tracking)

Integration with dual-backend SearchClient:

- Works with either Tantivy or SQLite (whichever client.search returns)
- Renders results identically regardless of backend
- Doesn't need to know which backend was used
- Filters translate correctly to both backends

The TUI now provides a complete user experience for searching agent conversation
history with sophisticated filtering, real-time results, and clear guidance when
the index is not yet built. This makes the tool immediately useful for exploring
large conversation archives.

Next enhancements could include:
- Arrow key result navigation with detail view
- Pagination controls (PgUp/PgDown, jump to page)
- Search-as-you-type for query field (remove Enter requirement)
- Fuzzy workspace/agent matching with autocomplete
- Result highlighting (show matching terms in snippet)
- Export results to file (JSON, CSV)
- Saved search queries (bookmarks)
